Skip to main content
Luminance

I'm Brian Hinton, accessibility lead at Superhuman and formerly at Grammarly. I spend most of my time thinking about what happens when you treat design as a form of care: for the people using the software, for the engineers building it, and for the systems that hold it all together.

For years my online presence was rented space. Social profiles and digital CVs on platforms I didn't own. This site is me building a home on the internet instead. The foundation is my own, the creaky floorboards are ones I coded, and the front door is always open. It's a place to slow down and share ideas that invite reflection rather than consumption, on design systems, accessibility, leadership, and whatever I'm paying attention to now.

Hi, I'm Brian Hinton. I lead accessibility at a company called Superhuman. Before that I worked at Grammarly. I care about making software that works for everyone.

This website is my own place on the internet. I built it myself. It is where I share my writing and ideas about design, accessibility, and leading teams. I also share what I am working on right now.

Now What I am doing right now

Right now I'm [ Focusing ] on building out Superhuman's accessibility program, defining audit processes, mentoring engineers on ARIA patterns, and shipping keyboard navigation improvements across the app. I'm also [ Listening ] to CPR by Wet Leg, while [ Reading ] The Oyster by Mark Kurlansky. Most recently [ Watching ] Hail Mary, in theaters now.

Working on: making Superhuman easier to use for people who navigate with keyboards and screen readers.
Listening to: a fun song called CPR by a band named Wet Leg.
Reading: a book about oysters and how they changed the world, by Mark Kurlansky.
Watching: a new movie called Hail Mary.

Work Things I made

Superhuman accessibility program Making Superhuman work for everyone Accessibility // Design systems Accessibility Cross-platform token system One design language for all platforms Design systems // Tooling Design systems Typographic space Playing with letters and shapes Exploration // Visual Art project

Writing Things I wrote

The weight of invisible decisions Choices people never see March 12, 2026 Accessibility is a management problem Accessibility needs good managers Jan 05, 2026 Managing designers without managing design Leading without taking over Nov 22, 2025
